steps.title: Find circle H radius
steps.content:
In standard circle form, the right side is the radius squared.
$$(x+5)^2+(y-4)^2=49$$
So
$$r_H^2=49$$
and $r_H=7$.

steps.title: Find circle J radius
steps.content:
Circle $J$ has radius $3$ units greater than circle $H$.
$$r_J=7+3=10$$

steps.title: Square the new radius
steps.content:
For circle $J$, the right side of the equation is $r_J^2$.
$$p=10^2=100$$
The answer is 100.
